intended_use: The iCAS-LV is intended to receive multi-phase volume datasets of reconstructed studies from PACS devices, to process them and to transfer the processing output to the PACS in DICOM format. It is a PC
intended_use_full: The iCAS-LV is intended to receive multi-phase volume datasets of reconstructed studies from PACS devices, to process them and to transfer the processing output to the PACS in DICOM format. It is a PC-based, self-contained, noninvasive image analysis software application. The device provides tools for visualization, measurements, segmentation, annotation, images registration, processing, and reporting. The device is intended for use by trained physicians. Further, the iCAS-LV is indicated to support the physicians in visualization of CT reconstructed images and evaluation of physician-identified liver lesions. The combination of the visualization, interactive segmentation, measurements, automatic registration, and volumetric analysis, supports the physician in evaluation of the lesions in terms of size, shape, position and changes over time. The iCAS should not be used in isolation for diagnosis and making patient management decisions.
target_condition: Liver lesions (metastases) on contrast-enhanced CT imaging
ground_truth: Ground truth established by two experienced radiologists (one US board-certified) independently identifying and delineating liver metastases, with a third senior radiologist reviewing and finalizing delineations
other_metrics: DICE (Sørensen–Dice coefficient), ASSD (Average Symmetric Surface Distance), SHD (Symmetric Hausdorff Distance), volume agreement, RECIST (Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors) measurements. Phantom testing showed algorithm sphere volume estimation and diameter (mm) estimates comparable to or better than expert radiologists. Clinical testing demonstrated iCAS 3D volumetric assessment agrees with radiologist manual assessment.
